items.title: Trump unhappy with Iranian proposal, says US official
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A US official said on Monday that President Donald Trump is unhappy with an Iranian proposal because it did not address Iran's nuclear programme. "He doesn't love the proposal," the US official said, referring to Trump. Earlier in the day, Trump discussed the proposal with his top national security aides.

items.title: Anwar: N. Sembilan state election unlikely amid political uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said a state election in Negeri Sembilan is unlikely amid ongoing political uncertainty in the state. Earlier, the prime minister said any developments concerning the Negeri Sembilan state administration would be announced in due course. Meanwhile, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said he would meet Anwar soon to discuss the matter.

items.title: ‘The rules will be made in Malaysia’: Anwar warns against AI becoming tool for Western influence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned that Artificial Intelligence (AI) and major technology firms risk being used as instruments of Western geopolitical dominance, rather than neutral commercial tools. Speaking at the launch of the Huawei AI Lab and Innovation Centre at The Exchange 106 in TRX, Anwar said parts of the Western tech industry view AI not just as a business sector, but as part of a broader competition for global supremacy. “We are confronted with some views, a very dogmatic doctrine that certain forms of governance, certain forms of technology, found in the West must dictate our policies,” he said.

items.title: Technical breakouts signal further upside for selected Bursa counters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several stocks on Bursa Malaysia are showing signs of renewed bullish momentum, with several analysts highlighting potential upside following key technical breakouts. RHB Investment Bank noted that Sunway REIT has entered a bullish phase after breaking past a key resistance level, supported by strong price action. After climbing above the 39 sen resistance, the stock may extend the upside movement towards the 42 sen mark, followed by the 45 sen level," it said.

items.title: Oil prices rise as no end to Iran war stand-off seems in sight
items.summary: US President Donald Trump is unhappy with the latest Iranian proposal aimed at ending the war, a US official said on Monday. Trump's displeasure with the Iranian offer leaves the conflict deadlocked, with Iran shutting shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz, which typically carries supply equal to about 20 per cent of global oil and gas consumption, and the US keeping in place its blockade of Iranian ports. US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude for June rose 58 cents, or 0.6 per cent, to US$96.96, after gaining 2.1 per cent in the previous session.

items.title: Dr Akmal: No backdoor deals — Umno must choose dignity or polls as N. Sembilan crisis deepens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has doubled down on his opposition to any “backdoor government”, saying his stance has remained unchanged since the Sheraton Move, while taking aim at what he described as inconsistent reactions to similar political crises. In a series of Facebook posts from last night and this morning, Akmal said he would continue to reject any administration formed without a clear electoral mandate. I oppose any backdoor government,” he wrote in an earlier post.

items.title: Eco World Kulai land sale strengthens data centre push, says RHB Research
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eco World Development Group Bhd's latest disposal of its final data centre-designated land parcel in Kulai at a record-high price is expected to strengthen its financial capacity for future growth, said RHB Research. Last Friday, Eco World entered into a conditional sale-and-purchase agreement to sell 20.07 hectares of land at Quantum Edge (QE) industrial park in Kulai to a regional hyperscale data centre specialist, KNBDC Malaysia Five, for RM280.8 million. In total, Eco World has booked RM907 million worth of land sales to three data centre players within two years, compared to its land cost of only RM211 million for the 163.49-hectare piece of land at QE.

items.title: Ringgit mostly higher against major, Asian currencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit traded mostly higher against other major and Asian currencies in early trade today amid cautious sentiment in the currency market. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit was expected to trade within a narrow range today amid cautious market sentiment. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Stable India growth outlook masks hit to vast informal sector, say economists
items.summary: BENGALURU: India's economic growth outlook is broadly unchanged despite the US-Israeli war with Iran, according to a Reuters poll of economists who warned that data will not capture an already notable hit to the country's vast informal sector. India's gross domestic product is expected to grow 6.7 per cent this fiscal year, according to an April 20-27 Reuters poll of 54 economists, in line with March's forecast. Forecasts for fiscal 2026-27 ranged from 5.9 per cent to 7.5 per cent.

items.title: Police seek public help finding missing girl 15-year-old Noor Atikah
items.summary: KAJANG: Police are seeking public assistance in locating a 15-year-old girl, Noor Atikah Mohd Aiman, who has been reported missing since April 19. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received a report regarding the teenager's disappearance at 6.32pm on April 19. Members of the public with any information regarding Noor Atikah's whereabouts are urged to contact the nearest police station or the Kajang police headquarters at 03-89114222.

items.title: Bank of Japan set to keep rates steady as Iran war clouds outlook
items.summary: TOKYO: The Bank of Japan (BOJ) is widely expected to hold off raising interest rates on Tuesday, but drop hawkish signals to leave itself scope to push up borrowing costs in coming months to counter inflationary pressure from the Middle East conflict. The US-Israeli war with Iran has complicated the BOJ's efforts to raise still-low interest rates gradually to levels deemed neutral to the economy, seen by markets at around 1.5 per cent. At a two-day meeting ending on Tuesday, the central bank is widely expected to keep its short-term policy rate steady at 0.75 per cent as fading prospects of a near-term end to the Iran war keep markets volatile.

items.title: Retired teacher loses RM1mil, including home, in phone scam
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim was first contacted by a person claiming to be a "Shopee representative" on Sept 6, 2024, before the call was transferred to another person posing as a police officer. In a desperate bid to avoid arrest, said Yahaya, the victim made 122 transactions totalling RM1.01 million into several bank accounts between Sept 6, 2024, and April 5, 2026. The victim lodged a police report after realising she had been cheated," he said.

items.title: At UN, China denounces Japan and EU over South China Sea remarks
items.summary: NEW YORK: China denounced remarks by Japan and the European Union about the South China Sea at a UN Security Council meeting on Monday and accused Tokyo of provocative behaviour in the Taiwan Strait and planning military expansion. Neither mentioned China – which claims most of the South China Sea and disputes territory with Japan and in the East China Sea – by name. China's deputy UN ambassador Sun Lei called the Japanese remarks "unwarranted," saying they "completely confound black and white".

items.title: Trump, Melania slam Kimmel for 'widow' joke
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump called Monday for comedian Jimmy Kimmel to be fired for likening First Lady Melania Trump to an "expectant widow," in a joke made days before a third alleged attempt to assassinate Trump. Melania Trump herself had earlier lashed out at Kimmel in a rare statement, calling on US broadcaster ABC to "take a stand" against the late-night host over his comments. Kimmel made the remarks last Thursday, before the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington on Saturday which Trump attended.

items.title: Academician: Aminuddin remains Negri Sembilan MB unless proven to have lost majority
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun remains constitutionally legitimate despite competing political claims, an academician said. Associate Professor Dr Azeem Fazwan Ahmad Farouk said the key issue is not public declarations but whether there is formal proof that Aminuddin has lost majority support in the Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly. He said the most established method of determining loss of confidence remains a formal vote in the state assembly.

items.title: LRT3 main contractor faces over RM800mil in delay costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The main contractor the Light Rail Transit Line 3 (LRT3) could face over RM800 million fine as delays in the final stages of completion continue to accumulate. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the final enforceable penalty, or liquidated and ascertained damage (LAD), amount against Setia Utama LRT 3 Sdn Bhd will only be determined upon project completion, after which the standard dispute resolution process will apply. Loke told Business Times via a statement that the LAD mechanism exists for good reason, and accountability must be upheld across the delivery chain.

items.title: Teoh Hock Siew, a towering editorial veteran at Business Times
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: At a time when business journalism in Malaysia was still finding its footing, Business Times emerged as a key platform shaping how the country understood corporate and economic developments. First published on Oct 4, 1976, Business Times was built by a small but driven newsroom. The paper brought together a mix of local and international editorial talent to establish Malaysia's first full-fledged business daily.

items.title: Shares of Padini, Cropmate under pressure amid MACC probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ongoing probe by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on Padini Holdings Bhd and Cropmate Bhd have sparked selling pressures in the stocks on Monday. Padini's share price dropped nearly 10 per cent to hit an intraday low of RM1.40, a level last seen in November 2020. Year-to-date, the stock has dropped 19.55 per cent while on a one-year basis, Padini's shares have dived 30.77 per cent.

items.title: 4 killed, 51 injured in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon
items.summary: BEIRUT: At least four people were killed and 51 injured in Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon on Monday, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing local media. Lebanon's National News Agency (NNA) reported that a woman was among the dead, while the wounded include three children and six women. The agency also said the Israeli army conducted demolition operations in Hanin and carried out another large-scale blast in Chihine.

items.title: Britain's King Charles meets Trump in bid to salvage ties
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Britain's King Charles III met US President Donald Trump at the White House Monday, kicking off a high-stakes state visit shadowed by transatlantic tensions and a new alleged attempt to assassinate the US president. Behind the warm welcome for Charles and Queen Camilla in front of the cameras lay a deepening rift in the so-called "special relationship" between Washington and London over Trump's war in Iran. First Lady Melania Trump, wearing a primrose yellow suit, gave Charles and Camilla kisses on both cheeks.

items.title: Safety, easing traffic flow our top priorities, says Lingkaran Cekap
items.summary: PAHANG: Lingkaran Cekap Sdn Bhd has said safety and traffic flow remain top priorities as major development projects transform Genting Highlands. Addressing concerns over potential gridlock on the mountain's main road, the company said its traffic management plans were under constant review. On slope safety, the company said it deployed a monitoring network with specialised sensors and instruments to track ground movement across the mountain.

items.title: Watchdogs warn of highland project risks [WATCH]
items.summary: BENTONG: Environmental watchdogs have warned against ongoing development in Genting Highlands and Cameron Highlands, citing risks to slope stability, biodiversity and resources. Sahabat Alam Malaysia president Meena Raman said Genting Highlands is a highly environmentally sensitive area at an elevation of 1,800m, with many slopes exceeding 25 degrees. "Because of the environmentally sensitive nature of Genting Highlands, development should not be allowed willy-nilly.

items.title: Negri Sembilan faces leadership change or fresh polls amid tensions, say analysts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Analysts said the Negri Sembilan crisis could lead to a change of menteri besar, renewed coalition talks, or even a state election. Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said maintaining the coalition and appointing a new menteri besar could help restore calm. "This could reduce greater political risks."

items.title: When the customers stay home: WFH policy leaves eateries near government offices facing quieter days
items.summary: Usually, stall owners say the medan selera or food courts near the Jalan Duta government complex are bustling during breakfast and lunchtime. Since the work-from-home directive for civil servants began — introduced to conserve energy amid shifts linked to the Middle East conflict — the change has been immediate for food stall owners. Siti Kulsum Ambrin, 40, who runs a stall selling Western cuisine, said the drop in business was drastic.

items.title: Iraqi president nominates Ali al-Zaidi as PM-designate
items.summary: BAGHDAD: Iraq's newly elected president nominated businessman Ali al-Zaidi as the country's prime minister-designate on Monday, after the country's leaders yielded to US pressure not to support the bid of a former premier close to Iran. The Coordination Framework, an alliance of Shia factions with varying links to Iran, had initially backed powerbroker Nouri al-Maliki to become the country's next premier, but an ultimatum by US President Donald Trump left Iraqi leaders looking elsewhere. "President Nizar Amede has tasked Ali al-Zaidi, the candidate of the largest parliamentary bloc, with forming the new government," the presidency said in a statement.

items.title: Trains collide near Jakarta, killing five, injuring dozens
items.summary: BEKASI, Indonesia: Two trains collided outside the Indonesian capital Jakarta late Monday, killing at least five people, injuring dozens, and prompting a mammoth rescue effort. One survivor told AFP of the horrific moments after a long-distance train slammed into the stationary commuter train she was in, trapping people inside mangled carriages. "There were two announcements from the commuter train.

items.title: Accused media gala gunman charged with attempting to assassinate Trump
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A man accused of trying to open fire on a media gala attended by Donald Trump in Washington this weekend was charged in court Monday with attempting to assassinate the Republican president. Cole Allen, 31, faces a sentence of up to life in prison if convicted of attempting to kill Trump during the White House Correspondents Association dinner at a hotel in the capital. The California man's attack was the third attempt on 79-year-old Trump's life in two years.

items.title: Iran FM blames US for failure of talks as he meets Putin
items.summary: SAINT PETERSBURG: Iran's top diplomat blamed Washington Monday for the failure of Middle East peace talks during a visit to Russia, where President Vladimir Putin promised him Moscow's support in ending the war. Iran's envoy to the UN said Teheran would first need guarantees Washington and Israel would not attack again if it was to offer security assurances in the Gulf. Araghchi said the war with the United States and Israel showed "Iran's true power" and stability.

items.title: Trump and team renew attacks on adversaries after gala shooting
items.summary: WASHINGTON: For a brief moment, US President Donald Trump spoke of "love and coming together" after a shooting at a media gala. Less than 48 hours after Saturday's incident, at which thousands of journalists were present, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt was blaming the alleged assassination attempt on "systemic demonisation" by Trump's opponents. Trump has often responded aggressively to female journalists in particular, calling one "piggy."

items.title: US, Iran clash at UN after Teheran gets nuclear non-proliferation role
items.summary: NEW YORK: The US and Iran clashed at the United Nations on Monday over Teheran's nuclear programme and its selection to be one of dozens of vice-presidents at a month-long conference to review the nuclear non-proliferation treaty. Christopher Yeaw, assistant secretary for the US Bureau of Arms Control and Nonproliferation, told the conference that Iran's selection was an "affront" to the NPT. Iran has insisted it does not seek nuclear weapons.

items.title: RM26m allocated for affordable meals at universities
items.summary: The Menu Kasih Siswa programme by the Higher Education Ministry has started, now with a higher allocation of RM26 million. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said university administrators should not wait for the minister to launch the initiative before rolling it out. He said the ministry would extend the programme to private higher education institutions.

items.title: 'MMC has resolved 5 of 7 complaints involving media'
items.summary: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has received seven complaints since its establishment last June. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said five complaints had been resolved and two were under review. She said the MMC served as the main platform for handling public complaints involving the media.

items.title: US airlines seek US$2.5b aid package
items.summary: NEW YORK: A coalition of US low-cost carriers, including Frontier and Avelo, is seeking US$2.5 billion in government assistance to offset soaring fuel costs, according to the Wall Street Journal. The group has reportedly offered the US government warrants convertible into equity stakes in exchange for the aid. During the Covid-19 pandemic, the US Treasury received warrants in major airlines in exchange for aid under a US$54 billion support programme.

items.title: Final talks begin on missing piece for pandemic treaty
items.summary: The agreement's Pathogen Access and Benefit-Sharing system deals with sharing access to pathogens with pandemic potential, then sharing the benefits derived from them: vaccines, tests and treatments. "Developing countries fear they will share their viruses without any guarantees of equitable access to vaccines in a crisis," said WHO chief scientist Sylvie Briand. Another challenge, said Briand, was "to integrate the sharing of genetic data for developing vaccines, treatments and diagnostics".

items.title: Loke: DAP rejects ‘backdoor government’ in Negeri Sembilan, says constitution must prevail
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 27 — Negeri Sembilan DAP will not support any “backdoor government” in the state, DAP national secretary-general Anthony Loke said today. He said attempts to install another administration outside the current one would disregard the people’s mandate from the last state election. “At the same time, any question over the Menteri Besar’s position must be tested in the State Legislative Assembly,” Loke told a press conference at the Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar’s official residence here.

items.title: The Negeri Sembilan political crisis: What we know so far
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Negeri Sembilan remains in a state of political paralysis as a rare royal constitutional dispute has snowballed into a power tussle that threatens to end Menteri Besar (MB) Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s leadership. All 14 Umno state assemblymen officially declared a loss of confidence in Aminuddin, accusing him of “mishandling” the royal crisis and failing to respect traditional institutions. Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias with other Umno assemblymen during a press conference declaring their withdrawal of support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun in Seremban April 27, 2026.

items.title: ‘Later’: PM declines to comment on Negeri Sembilan turmoil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he would address the current political crisis in Negeri Sembilan at a later time. “Just now Pakatan Harapan and the Menteri Besar (of Negeri Sembilan) already did a press conference on it,” Anwar told the press. Earlier today, Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he would remain in office after His Royal Highness the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, advised him to continue administering the state.

items.title: Umno reps claim simple majority to form N. Sembilan government with five Perikatan assemblymen
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 27 — Negeri Sembilan Umno and Barisan Nasional today declared that it has obtained a simple majority to form the state government through cooperation with five Perikatan Nasional assemblymen. “Umno-BN has obtained a simple majority to form the state government,” they said. The statement followed a media statement issued by Negeri Sembilan PN assemblymen earlier today over the political crisis affecting the state government.

items.title: MB says Umno reps should have just tabled no-confidence motion in N. Sembilan assembly
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 27 — The Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun today said any challenge to his leadership should be tested through a motion of no confidence in the State Legislative Assembly. He said the Barisan Nasional bloc should have let the sitting go on as usual (today). “For me, at that time, the presence of 35 assemblymen showed support for Tuanku (Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir), support for the Dewan Undangan Negeri (State Legislative Assembly), and support for the sitting to continue,” he said.

items.title: PM Anwar launches Huawei AI Lab and Innovation Centre at The Exchange 106 in TRX
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim launched the new Huawei AI Lab and Innovation Centre in The Exhange 106 at TRX Kuala Lumpur today. He also congratulated the Chinese tech company on their 25th year anniversary in Malaysia while also thanking them for their continuous confidence and support for Malaysia. Huawei has been in Malaysia since 2001 with the company registered as Huawei Technologies (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Zahid says 14 Umno reps independently withdrew support for N. Sembilan govt, central leadership to make final call
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — The decision by 14 Umno assemblymen to withdraw support from the Negeri Sembilan state government was made by the state chapter independently, party president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. “However, the final verdict on the matter will ultimately come from us (Umno’s central leadership).” Zahid added that he will meet the 14 Umno assemblymen involved and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim soon to discuss the political crisis unfolding in Negeri Sembilan. The deputy prime minister said that Umno usually respects and accommodates decisions made by state Umno liaison bodies.

items.title: Umno to discuss possibility of early general election, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the party has yet to decide whether it will push for an early general election. “Whether Umno wants an early general election, that is something we will discuss,” he said. On April 24, Bloomberg reported that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is considering the possibility of holding a general election in the third quarter, as his administration evaluates politically sensitive cuts to fuel subsidies amid continued global energy pressures.

items.title: Perak vows no compromise after alleged bullying at MRSM over damaged student clothing
items.summary: IPOH, April 27 — The Perak state government has stressed that it will not compromise on any form of bullying involving students at a MARA Junior Science College (MRSM) in the state, following an incident where students were alleged to have cut and damaged another student’s clothing. Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Khairudin Abu Hanipah said those involved in the incident would face appropriate action. “In my view, if the students involved in this bullying case are found guilty, they must accept punishment in accordance with the rules and circulars issued by the school and the Education Ministry,” he told reporters after an education programme, which was also attended by Perak State Education Department director Zulkafli Mohamed Mokhtar here today.

items.title: Farmer in Kota Tinggi loses RM738,950 after falling victim to bogus ‘AI Investment’ scam
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 27 — A 59-year-old farmer lost RM738,950 after falling victim to an online investment scam known as the “AI Investment” scheme. Kota Tinggi police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the victim was approached in February by an unknown woman via WhatsApp, who lured him with promises of returns worth millions of ringgit. Believing the offer to be genuine, the victim subsequently made five money transfers to four different bank accounts.

items.title: Terengganu peat fire contained, schools in Kuala Abang continue sessions as smoke clears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Firefighters have brought a peat swamp forest fire in Kuala Abang in Dungun, Terengganu, under control, allowing more than 1,000 students from nearby schools to continue their classes as smoke conditions improved. In a statement, Dungun Fire and Rescue station chief Yahaya Hanafi said visibility and air quality around the affected schools had improved significantly following intensified suppression efforts carried out near the fire zone, Harian Metro reported. He said a total of 1,024 students from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama (SMKA) Kuala Abang and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Kampung Baru Kuala Abang were initially at risk due to the nearby blaze.

items.title: N. Sembilan MB to remain in office after 14 Umno assemblymen withdraw support
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 27 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will continue in office after the Royal Highness Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir has advised him to carry on administering the state. His clarification comes after 14 Umno assemblymen withdrew their support for the state government and his leadership as Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar. Earlier today, Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said all 14 Umno assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan had lost confidence in Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and withdrawn their support for the state government under his leadership.

items.title: Farmer in Terengganu loses RM300,000 after bushfire razes chilli fertigation plot
items.summary: Wan Mohd Nazmi Wan Mohd Najib, 40, said the April 9 incident was the first he had experienced since he began operating the suspended fertigation system six years ago. “Upon arriving at the plot, I found the fire raging, consuming the grass, support pillars, and fertigation polybags. The fire destroyed wooden support poles and scorched 10,000 planting polybags across a 1.2-hectare area.

items.title: Cancer care demands rising, Health Minister highlights shortage of oncologists despite workforce increase
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Cancer remains the third leading cause of death in Malaysia, with about 40,000 new cases recorded annually, underscoring mounting pressure on the country’s healthcare system and workforce capacity. Cancer remains the third highest cause of death in Malaysia, with approximately 40,000 new cases reported each year. Organised by Roche Malaysia, the inaugural forum brought together women oncologists nationwide to exchange perspectives on mentorship, leadership and professional development in cancer care.

items.title: Selangor Council of the Royal Court condemns alleged insulting post targeting Sultan, urges stern action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — The Selangor Council of the Royal Court has strongly condemned a social media post alleged to have insulted the Sultan of Selangor, describing it as irresponsible and capable of disrupting public harmony. The council said such actions were “provocative and uncivilised”, adding that they constituted a serious insult to the royal institution and could threaten unity among Malaysia’s multiracial society. The council also called for firm action to be taken against those responsible, urging authorities including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to act without compromise.

items.title: King honours RMN personnel and veterans on 92nd anniversary, extends prayers for fallen servicemen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today conveyed his greetings on the 92nd anniversary of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) to its personnel, veterans and those who have served in the force. His Majesty, as the Supreme Commander of the Malaysian Armed Forces, also expressed his most profound gratitude for the dedication, service and sacrifices made by all RMN personnel and veterans in safeguarding the nation’s sovereignty and security. A parade in conjunction with the 92nd RMN Day celebration and themed “TLDM Perkasa Kedaulatan Terpelihara” (RMN Empowered, Sovereignty Preserved) was held at the Lumut Naval Base, Perak today.

items.title: Federal Court to decide prosecution’s appeal in Syed Saddiq acquittal on June 30
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — The Federal Court has fixed June 30 to deliver its decision on the prosecution’s appeal against the acquittal of Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman over four charges linked to funds from Armada. The outcome — whether Syed Saddiq remains acquitted or is convicted — will be decided by Court of Appeal president Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais, who will sit with Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Razali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, after having heard submissions from both sides. The prosecution in the appeal is led by Wan Shaharuddin, alongside Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib and Farah Ezlin Yusop Khan, while Syed Saddiq, 33, is represented by lawyer Hisyam Teh Poh Teik.

items.title: National Workers’ Day carnival promises 4,000 job vacancies, skills development and support services for workers
items.summary: The Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma) said the celebration, themed ‘Pekerja Madani Kesuma Bangsa,’ will be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and is expected to gather more than 15,000 workers from various sectors, including trade unions, statutory bodies, as well as the public and private sectors. “The highlight of the event will feature a special address by the Prime Minister, along with the presentation of the 2026 National Workers’ Day Awards in recognition of workers’ contributions to national development,” Kesuma said in a statement today. Kesuma said that throughout the three-day event, 2026 National Workers’ Day carnival is expected to attract up to 30,000 visitors, focusing on the needs of the public across the entire career ecosystem, including career entry, existing workers, career advancement through skills upgrading, as well as public well-being through access to various support services.

items.title: ‘Tok Min is far better than me’: Rafizi praises Aminuddin’s humility, prays for MB and wife
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 27 — Former minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has voiced support for Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, describing him as “like an elder brother” and praising his humility despite years in office. In a post on X, Rafizi said he had called Aminuddin in the morning to check in. Rafizi added that he was not worried about Aminuddin, whom he affectionately referred to as “Tok Min”, calling him “a good man, far better than me”.

items.title: Shafee refuses to elaborate on Najib’s legal moves, says matters beyond him
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s lawyer, Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, has declined to comment on developments involving the former prime minister, including the withdrawal of his appeal over an alleged addendum order linked to a house arrest bid and reports of a fresh application for royal pardon. Speaking to reporters after proceedings at the Court of Appeal, Muhammad Shafee said the matters were beyond his purview. When pressed further, he reiterated: “The correct quotation is, I cannot comment.” Earlier today, court documents revealed that Najib had withdrawn his appeal against the High Court’s decision dismissing his application for judicial review over an alleged addendum order.

items.title: Court finds doubt in charge details, ex‑school van driver cleared of sexual assault
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — A former school van driver was acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court here today on a charge of committing physical sexual assault against an eight-year-old boy at a condominium in 2022. Judge Datin Kunasundary Marimuthu freed Na Li Jing, 34, at the close of the defence case, finding that a reasonable doubt had been raised against the prosecution’s case. Are the premises stated in the charge correctly attributed to the accused?

items.title: Deputy CM says Pan Borneo project vital, calls for engagement on compensation concerns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 27 — Sabah Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam has urged all state assemblymen to engage with residents to explain the importance of the Pan Borneo Highway project, including issues surrounding compensation. Dr Joachim, who is also the state Works and Utilities Minister, said there have been cases where residents served with eviction notices have yet to receive compensation, due in part to incomplete documentation or disagreements over the amounts offered. But we must also acknowledge that the Pan Borneo project is a game-changer for Sabah, highly important, and some degree of sacrifice is required.

items.title: No fines for Haj pilgrims taking photos at Kaaba, Malaysian delegation clarifies
items.summary: MAKKAH, April 27 — Claims circulating on social media that Haj pilgrims will be fined up to 10,000 Saudi riyals for taking photos around the Kaaba are not true. According to him, pilgrims are still allowed to take photos around the Kaaba, but they must observe proper etiquette and avoid doing so while performing acts of worship. Earlier, a claim went viral on social media alleging that Saudi authorities would impose a fine of 10,000 Saudi riyals (about RM10,500), along with detention and deportation, on anyone taking photos or videos at Masjidil Haram, especially in front of the Kaaba.

items.title: Media converge on MB’s residence as emergency meetings follow Umno’s withdrawal of support in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Media personnel have begun gathering outside the official residence of Negeri Sembilan Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, following Umno’s decision to withdraw support for his administration. Separately, Negeri Sembilan DAP, which holds 11 seats, convened an emergency meeting at its state headquarters in Kemayan Square, chaired by secretary-general and state chairman Anthony Loke. Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has called an emergency meeting at his official residence in Seremban after Umno withdrew support for his administration in Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: Police report filed as society distances itself from fake account targeting Selangor Sultan
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — The Huayan Pureland Buddhist Society has lodged a police report after its name and Facebook account were misused to upload posts allegedly insulting the Sultan of Selangor. The police report, displayed at the society’s premises, stated that its 57‑year‑old secretary realised the impersonation after receiving a message on April 25. The secretary strongly denied any involvement in insulting the Sultan, describing the incident as a deliberate act of defamation.

items.title: Scam content takedowns hit 43,618 in Q1, Teo Nie Ching calls trend worrying
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — A total of 43,618 scam-related content was removed by social media platforms as of the first quarter of this year, said Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching. She said the removals were carried out at the request of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), with platform providers complying with the applications. Teo said the number of scam-related content taken down has risen sharply each year, with 6,297 cases recorded in 2023, increasing to 63,652 in 2024 and 98,503 in 2025.
